page: Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 205 GB of local storage, lacks any GPU accelerators, and does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the D12 is positioned in the lower tiers for raw compute tasks. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks register in the bottom 25%, while Geekbench single-core and compression tests (gzip and bzip3) fall into the bottom 10%. However, memory performance is more resilient; memory bandwidth tests and PassMark memory scores consistently rank in the middle 50%. Redis SET operations also achieve an average rating with 5.98 million operations per second, and small-model LLM inference maintains average throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the Azure D12 is its modest CPU compute capacity relative to its generous memory allocation. While it is not suited for CPU-intensive tasks like heavy compilation or ray tracing, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-frequency processing. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, medium-sized databases, caching layers like Redis, and lightweight web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 28.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage, with no GPU capabilities. Benchmark data shows weak CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. However, memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores remain average, and Redis SET operations reach 5.98 million operations per second. The instance presents a clear tradeoff: lower compute performance paired with a relatively high memory-to-core ratio. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-sensitive but compute-light applications. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, caching layers, and database operations that benefit from its 28.0 GB memory capacity rather than raw processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, offering balanced memory performance for general-purpose and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D12 is a virtual machine with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V.
tagline: A dedicated four-core x86_64 virtual machine featuring a generous memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated local storage for memory-centric workloads.

28.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
